items.label: Contact Us form (HubSpot)
items.notes: Rendered client-side by HubSpot's js.hsforms.net embed and submits to Hanover's HubSpot portal 3409306; on the clone it either posts to the original owner's HubSpot or is inert. A new owner needs their own HubSpot portal/form (or a form endpoint/mailto).

items.label: Newsletter / subscribe form (HubSpot)
items.notes: Same HubSpot newsletter form recurs site-wide (footer and /subscribe/). Renders via HubSpot embed JS and posts to Hanover's HubSpot portal; needs the owner's own HubSpot form or an email-list endpoint.

items.label: Become a Client / request-a-demo form (HubSpot)
items.notes: Lead/demo-request form embedded from HubSpot; posts to Hanover's HubSpot portal 3409306. Needs the owner's own HubSpot portal/form to capture leads.

items.label: Gated content download form (HubSpot) — report/resource pages
items.original_target: HubSpot Forms API — hbspt.forms.create({portalId:"3409306", formId:"c3dff425-806c-450a-a384-d814ed6352af"}) (formId varies per report/resource)
items.notes: Gated-asset download forms appear on report/brief/toolkit/resource pages, each with its own HubSpot formId, all under portal 3409306. Renders via HubSpot embed and gates a file download; needs the owner's own HubSpot form + asset delivery.

items.label: Webinar registration / recording-access form (HubSpot) — webinar & podcast pages
items.original_target: HubSpot Forms API — hbspt.forms.create({portalId:"3409306", formId:"2b25e781-d69f-4b69-a35d-83557d3631cc"}) (formId varies per webinar/podcast)
items.notes: Every webinar page (and the podcast pages) embeds a HubSpot form to register for / unlock the on-demand recording, each with its own formId under portal 3409306. Renders client-side from js.hsforms.net and submits to Hanover's HubSpot portal; on the clone it is inert and needs the owner's own HubSpot form + gated media delivery. Some webinars also link out to hanoverresearch.zoom.us (external, kept as-is).

items.label: Site search (Elementor + SearchWP)
items.notes: Site search box appears in the header on every page. On the static clone the form action was neutralised (empty action) so it returns no results page; needs a real search backend or a hosted search service to function.
